name: email_verify_link

metadata.description: Email address verification link (sent by on_email_verify_send when the user requests verification from the public/anonymous flow).
notes: `user` is a dict (user.to_dict('basic')) auto-injected by User.send_template_email if missing.
This framework does not define the frontend verification URL. Include a placeholder link pattern that projects can customize.
Token has a 24-hour TTL and is single-use.

text_template:
Hello{% if user.display_name %} {{ user.display_name }}{% endif %},

Please verify your email address using the token below.

Verification token:
{{ token }}

If your project supports verification links, your URL typically looks like:
https://YOUR_APP_HOST/verify-email?token={{ token }}

This link expires in 24 hours.

If you didn't request this, you can safely ignore this email.
html_template:
Verify your email address

Hello{% if user.display_name %} {{ user.display_name }}{% endif %},

Please verify your email address by using the token below.

Verification token
{{ token }}

If your project supports verification links, your URL typically looks like:
https://YOUR_APP_HOST/verify-email?token={{ token }}

This link expires in 24 hours.

If you didn't request this, you can safely ignore this email.

Sent by Django-MOJO
